skill.name: data-storytelling
skill.description: Transform analytics into clear stakeholder narratives using story structure, visual framing, and action-oriented recommendations.
skill.summary: Turn raw metrics into persuasive data stories for reports, dashboards, and executive presentations.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown code fences, external resource links, and business example text as risky patterns. Review found these are documentation examples only, with no executable scripts, command execution, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged repeated triple backtick markdown fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The cited lines introduce narrative templates, markdown examples, and a matplotlib illustration inside SKILL.md; they are not executable skill code and do not run external commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line-numbered review shows these are fenced markdown or example blocks, not shell invocation syntax. No script file or command runner is present in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs Are Documentation Links Only
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged three hardcoded URLs in the Resources section. They are ordinary markdown links to public data storytelling references and do not transmit user data or initiate network requests by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in markdown list items under Resources. There is no code path that fetches these URLs or sends local data to them.

low_findings.title: Business Text Misclassified as Blocker Patterns
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and system reconnaissance terms, but the cited lines contain frontmatter and business analysis examples. No cryptographic algorithm, host inspection, process listing, or environment probing behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is descriptive content such as conversion metrics, recommendations, and a book link. No semantic evidence supports cryptographic weakness or reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: Raw metrics often fail to persuade stakeholders or support decisions. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code shape analysis into clear narratives, visuals, and recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Structures analytics into setup, conflict, resolution, and action-oriented recommendations.
Provides reusable frameworks for problem-solution, trend, and comparison stories.
Suggests chart annotations, progressive reveal sequences, and contrast techniques.
Creates executive summary, dashboard story, and presentation flow outlines.
Improves data headlines, transitions, uncertainty framing, and call-to-action language.
limitations: Does not validate source data quality or statistical correctness.
Does not generate finished slide decks or live dashboard files by itself.
Requires accurate metrics, audience context, and business goals from the user.
Visualization code examples are illustrative and may need adaptation for real datasets.

use_cases.title: Executive Business Review
use_cases.description: Turn quarterly metrics into a concise narrative with key insight, root cause, recommendation, and decision request.

use_cases.title: Investor Update Narrative
use_cases.description: Frame growth, risks, and market opportunity with clear comparisons and a persuasive forward-looking story.

use_cases.title: Product Analytics Readout
use_cases.description: Explain adoption, retention, or funnel data to non-technical teams with focused visuals and plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Data Story
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple narrative structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to turn these metrics into a short data story for a non-technical audience: [paste metrics]. Include the key insight, supporting evidence, and next step.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Executive Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create an executive summary slide outline from this analysis: [paste analysis]. Lead with the business impact and end with a clear decision request.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Presentation Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a full stakeholder presentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a 7-slide data storytelling flow for [audience] about [topic]. Include the hook, context, discovery, deep dive, recommendation, impact, and ask.

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Complex Narrative
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request to improve persuasion and uncertainty handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to critique and improve this data narrative: [paste draft]. Strengthen the headline, clarify uncertainty, reduce jargon, and make the recommendation more actionable.

output_examples.input: Monthly revenue rose 15 percent, but customer acquisition cost rose 22 percent and trial conversion fell five points.
output_examples.output: Headline: Revenue is growing, but acquisition efficiency is weakening. Story flow: show revenue momentum, reveal the CAC pressure, isolate the conversion drop, then recommend reallocating spend and testing onboarding changes.

output_examples.input: A team needs to choose between two international markets with different growth, competition, and regulatory profiles.
output_examples.output: The output frames the decision as a comparison story. It ranks market size, growth, competition, and operating ease, then recommends the stronger first market with risk mitigations.

output_examples.input: A dashboard has too many metrics and no clear conclusion for executives.
output_examples.output: The output identifies one primary insight, groups supporting metrics into what works and what needs attention, and ends with a focused next-month action plan.

best_practices: Start with the decision or insight before showing supporting details.
Match the level of detail to the audience and their decision authority.
State uncertainty, assumptions, and limitations directly when evidence is incomplete.
anti_patterns: Do not paste every metric without choosing a main message.
Do not lead with methodology when stakeholders need the business implication first.
Do not overstate causation when the data only supports correlation.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create narratives, presentation outlines, dashboard summaries, and recommendation stories based on business data.

faq.question: Can it analyze my raw dataset automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides the storytelling structure after you provide metrics, findings, or analysis results.

faq.question: Is this useful for executives?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill emphasizes concise insights, business impact, and clear decision requests for leadership audiences.

faq.question: Does it create charts?
faq.answer: It suggests chart types, annotations, and reveal sequences. Finished chart production depends on the tools and data you provide.

faq.question: How should I prepare a prompt?
faq.answer: Provide the audience, business question, key metrics, known constraints, and the decision you want to support.

faq.question: Can it handle uncertainty in data?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for confidence levels, ranges, caveats, and responsible interpretation of evidence.
